body:
In #22 another user commented on that issue tagging the agent, who currently does not have the needed roles.  What's a good ux for the maintainer to grant the access to the user? 

@sepo-agent /answer

comments.body:
A good UX here is a **maintainer-issued one-shot grant**, not a global policy change.

Current behavior is association-based: `AGENT_ACCESS_POLICY` allows routes by GitHub author association, and unauthorized route requests become `unsupported` with a reply like “answer requests currently require ... access” ([access-policy.ts](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.agent/src/access-policy.ts), [triage.ts](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.agent/src/triage.ts)). Approval comments already use a marker-driven flow with `@sepo-agent /approve <request_id>` ([supported-workflows.md](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.agent/docs/architecture/supported-workflows.md), [agent-approve.yml](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.github/workflows/agent-approve.yml)).

I’d extend that pattern:

1. When an external user is denied, the agent should post:
   ```md
   @user does not currently have access to run `/answer`.

   A maintainer can grant this request once with:

   @sepo-agent /grant <request_id>
   ```
   The comment should include a hidden `<!-- sepo-agent-grant-request ... -->` marker containing requester login, author association, route, source comment id/url, request text, target kind/number, and expiry.

2. A maintainer replies:
   ```md
   @sepo-agent /grant <request_id>
   ```
   The workflow validates the commenter against the existing access policy for a privileged route, probably `OWNER`, `MEMBER`, `COLLABORATOR` only.

3. If valid, the agent dispatches the original request exactly once, preserving:
   - original requester: `ltorroba`
   - granted by: maintainer login
   - grant source: approval comment URL
   - route: `answer`

4. The agent edits or replies to the denial/grant comment:
   ```md
   Granted by @maintainer. Running `/answer` for @ltorroba.
   ```

5. The one-shot grant should expire, e.g. after 7 days, and be consumed after dispatch.

I would avoid making this “add the user to `AGENT_ACCESS_POLICY`” because that policy is association-based today, not user-based, and widening it to `NONE` / `FIRST_TIMER` would grant every external user. I’d also avoid asking maintainers to add collaborators just to let someone ask the agent a question.

Implementation plan:

1. Add a grant-request marker type alongside the existing pending approval marker machinery.
2. Change unauthorized dispatch handling so it can emit a grantable denial marker instead of only plain unsupported text.
3. Add a grant resolver CLI similar to `resolve-approval.ts`, validating the grant command, marker, route, requester, expiry, and maintainer authorization.
4. Add `agent-grant.yml` triggered by `issue_comment` / `discussion_comment` containing `@sepo-agent /grant`.
5. Dispatch the original route with metadata showing both `requested_by` and `granted_by`.
6. Update docs in `access-policy.md` and `supported-workflows.md`.
7. Add tests for denied external user, authorized maintainer grant, unauthorized grant attempt, expired grant, and consumed grant.

That gives maintainers a visible, auditable, low-friction UX without permanently broadening repository access.

title: UX: design an explicit grant for external users to use the agent
